exercises.name: Double BOSU Dome Up Push Up
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain active abdominals and glutes. This will help you keep neutral spine throughout the exercise.

2) You’ll want to place the BOSU balls far enough apart that at the bottom of your push up, your elbows stay above your hands.

3) Put one of each of your hands in the center of either dome. Bring your feet back until your body is fully outstretched, and you are in a push up position. 

4) Slowly lower your chest towards the domes. Stop when your elbows reach a depth slightly past 90 degrees, and your chest opens slightly greater than 180 degrees. 

5) Push yourself back up to the original position, minimizing any rocking or wobbles by keeping your abdominal and glutes engaged. 

6) Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Double Deadbug
exercises.description: Similar to the deadbug, but with both arms and legs extended simultaneously, maintaining control and stability through the core.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Alternating Hang Clean
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a kettlebell in each hand with your arms straight, in between your feet.

2.Bend at the hips and knees slightly then thrust your hips forward pulling one kettlebell straight up, catching it at shoulder height, absorbing it by dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Lower this kettlebell back down in front then pull the other bell up to the opposite shoulder. 

4.Continue raising and lowering the bells in an alternating manner, keeping them close to your body throughout.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Arnold Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a kettlebell in each hand in the top position, resting at your biceps.

2.Looking straight ahead, press the kettlebell out and overhead.

3.Lower kettlebells down returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Clean
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a kettlebell in each hand between your feet, arms straight.

2.Thrust your hips forward pulling the kettlebells straight up, catching them at shoulder height, bending at the elbows. Absorb the kettlebells at your shoulders by dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Return to starting position. Keep the bells close to your body throughout.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Clean to Front Squat
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, kettlebells on the floor in front of you. Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees to grab the kettlebells. 

Explosively pull them up in a clean motion, bringing the kettlebells to shoulder height while keeping your elbows close to your body. 

Once at shoulder height, lower into a front squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest upright and core engaged. Push through your heels to return to standing.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Push Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright, feet about shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand in the top position, resting at your biceps.

2.Squat down a few inches.

3.Use the momentum from the legs to drive the kettlebells overhead, legs straight.

4.Once the kettlebells are locked out, lower the kettlebells to your shoulders returning to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Racked Front Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright, feet about shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand in the top position, resting at your biceps.

2.Looking straight ahead at all times, squat as low as you can and pause at the bottom.

3.Rise back up returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding kettlebells on each hand in between your legs, feet flat, shoulder-width apart.

2.Lower your body toward the floor, sending your hips back and down and bending your knees. 

3.Push through your heels to return to the start position, keeping your back flat and head up throughout the movement. 

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Double Kettlebell Swing
exercises.description:
1) Place two kettlebells between your feet. Push back with your butt, making sure that your back is flat and bend your knees to get into the starting position.

2) Swing the kettlebells between your legs forcefully. 

3) Quickly reverse the direction and drive through with your hips taking the kettlebells straight out to chest level. 

3) Let the kettlebells swing back between your legs.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Double Under
exercises.description:
1) The rope travels twice underneath you. 

2) Start the exercise with a single under to build momentum.

exercises.name: Dowel Around the World
exercises.description:
1) Place hands on dowel so it is waist height. 

2) With your arms remaining mostly straight, lift one arm overhead while leaving the other by your hip. 

3) As the top arm reaches overhead, and then behind your head, start the arm remaining by your hip in the same motion. 

4) The first moving arm will return to the starting hip as the second arm reaches the overhead position. 

5) Repeat for the prescribed repetitions.

exercises.name: Dowel Behind the Head Slides
exercises.description:
1) Place hands wider than shoulder width apart on the dowel. 

2) Put the dowel overhead with straight arms. 

3) Slowly bend your arms so that the dowel travels behind your head as you slide it down behind your body. Aim to allow the dowel to get mid back (as long as you aren't forcing or having to compensate position to get there). 

4) Glide your arms back up to the overhead position.

5) Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Dowel Overhead Squat
exercises.description:
1) Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart.

2) Place your hands wider than shoulder width apart on the dowel. Move the dowel to the overhead position, biceps in line with ears.

3) With your back in neutral spine, sit your hips as far as possible while maintaining your correct posture as you start to squat. 

4) As your body position moves to keep parallel to your shins during your squat, track your arms slightly behind your head in order to keep your arms and the dowel directly vertical over your body (perpendicular to the ground) at all times during the movement. 

5) Return to a standing position be pushing through your heels to activate your glutes and press to the standing position. 

6) Repeat for the prescribed repetitions.

exercises.name: Down Dog Single Leg Hip Opener to Climber
exercises.description:
Begin in downward dog position (hips lifted, arms and legs straight). 

Lift one leg toward the ceiling, then open your hips by rotating your leg outward. Hold for a stretch before switching legs. 

This targets hamstrings, hips, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Downward Dog
exercises.description:
1) When performing this mobility exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your knees stay straight.

2) From there, you’ll tip your hips up towards the ceiling.

3) Make a nice long line throughout your torso.

4) Stretch down the back of your thighs and your hamstrings. 

5) If that’s a little intense, you can alternate between the left and right side being locked out and straightened while the opposite leg is bent.

exercises.name: Downward Dog Crossbody Reach
exercises.description: In downward dog, reach one hand across your body toward the opposite ankle for a deeper stretch.

exercises.name: Downward Dog to Push-Up
exercises.description: Transition from downward dog into a push-up position, then return.

exercises.name: Downward Dog to Scorpion
exercises.description: From downward dog, lift one leg and bend the knee, opening the hip for a scorpion stretch.

exercises.name: Duck stance DB row into RDL
Duck Walk
exercises.description:
1) Tuck arms under armpits, or place your hands on top of your head. Squat down as low as you can, getting your hips below your knees. 

2) Without standing out of your squat, step by shifting your weight and rotating at your hips, allowing you to walk. 

3) Repeat for the given distance, or reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell 1.5 Rep Goblet Squat
exercises.alternateName: full rep half rep goblet squat
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell vertically at your chest with both hands, elbows pointing down. Step your feet shoulder-width apart. 

Lower your body into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Rise slightly halfway up (about one-quarter of the way), then lower back down into the squat position. 

Push through your heels to return fully to standing. Repeat this “squat, quarter-rise, squat” pattern for reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell 21
exercises.description:
1. Stand upright and grab dumbbells with both hands. Place your hands shoulders-width apart and allow your arms to hang toward the floor.

2. Tuck your elbows tight to the sides of your body.

3. Curl upward until you make a 90-degree angle at your elbows. Relax your arms back to full extension and repeat six more curls reaching the 90-degree angle at your elbow.

4. Now, from the 90-degree at your elbow position, curl the weight up until the dumbbells are one to two inches away from your shoulder.

5. Lower the weight back to the 90-degree elbow position and repeat six more times.

6. Now, allow your arms to return to full extension.

7. This time, curl your arms from full extension all the way to full extension. Keep curling until the bar is about one to two inches away from your shoulder.

8. Repeat six more curls through this full range of motion to complete a total of 21 reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell 6-Way Shoulder Raise
exercises.description:
Hold a dumbbell in each hand. Raise your arms straight in front to shoulder height, move them out to the sides, then overhead. 

Reverse the motion back to start,
maintaining control at all times.

exercises.name: Dumbbell 90 Degree Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding the dumbbells out in front with your elbows bent at 90 degrees.

2.Raise your arms up and outward to the sides to shoulder height, keeping your elbows bent throughout.

3.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand up with arms hanging and dumbbells in both hands, palms facing in. 

2) Curl dumbbell for one arm, twisting your wrist on the way up and keeping your elbows still. 

3) Slowly lower and alternate with the other arm

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on a bench with dumbbells in each hand, arms extended. 

Lower one dumbbell towards your chest while keeping the other arm extended, then press it back up and switch sides. 

This variation increases core engagement and stabilizer activation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Curtsy Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold dumbbells at your sides and stand with feet hip-width apart. 

Step one leg behind and across the other into a curtsy lunge, then return to standing and repeat on the other side. 

Alternate legs with each rep, maintaining balance.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Floor Press
exercises.description:
1.Lie on the floor holding dumbbells at shoulder level with your elbows bent and your palms facing forward.

2.Press one dumbbell up over your chest, arm straight, while holding the other stationary.

3.Lower the pressed dumbbell back to shoulder level and immediately press the dumbbell with your other arm.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Hammer Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand upright, holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging at your sides and your palms inwards.

2) Curl the weight in one hand towards the shoulder in a circular motion.

3) Squeeze the bicep at the end of the lift and slowly lower. 

4) Alternate hands and repeat. Keep you body still, elbows locked and palms faced inwards throughout the whole exercise

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Hammer Preacher Curl
exercises.description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, holding a dumbbell in each hand and arms resting on the pad.

2.Raise one dumbbell up to shoulder level, while leaving the other to starting position.

3.Alternate. Both dumbbells should be moving at the same time but in opposite directions.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Lateral Lunge to Press
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell Alternating Side Lunge to Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing inward. 

Step one foot out to the side into a lateral lunge, bending the lead knee while keeping the other leg straight. 

Push through the lead heel to return to the standing position, then press the dumbbells overhead. 

Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder level and immediately repeat the same sequence on the opposite side. Continue alternating sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Lateral Raise to Front Raise
exercises.alternateName: Side Raise to Front Raise shoulders
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, lift the weights out to the sides up to shoulder height, then lower them down. Next, lift the weights in front of you to shoulder height and lower them again. Repeat the side and front raises alternately.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Preacher Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up and arms resting on the pad.

2.Raise one dumbbell up to shoulder level, while leaving the other to starting position.

3.Alternate. Both dumbbells should be moving at the same time but in opposite directions.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
***Unlike the video hold dumbbells in your hands. And alternate every rep*** (updated video coming)

1) Step backward into a lunge position ensuring that your body is over top of your back knee. 

2) Keep all your joints in your lower body at 90 degrees at the bottom of the lunge. 

3) Step forward returning to the start position with feet side by side.  

4) Repeat by stepping back with the opposite foot to the side you just did.

5) Repeat alternating sides.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Reverse Lunge Curl to Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, alternate stepping back into reverse lunges, curl the weights to your shoulders, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Reverse Lunge into Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, alternate stepping back into reverse lunges, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Reverse Lunge to Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, alternate stepping back into reverse lunges, then press the weights overhead.
